analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (inferred: tensor_core_active_pct=0 on conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc depthwise kernels and registers_per_thread<=40 SIMT path; pointwise Kernel2 shows only partial TC engagement ~45%)

bottleneck.description: The prologue NVTX range (50.9% of attributed time) is dominated NOT by the convolutions themselves but by a long chain of standalone BatchNorm + activation elementwise kernels emitted around every conv. The repeated kernel names 'triton_poi_fused__native_batch_1...' / '_2...' / '_3...' / '_4...' (grids 1568, 3136, 12544, [98,128]) are separate launches of the inference-time BatchNorm normalization and its broadcast epilogue, run as their own memory-bound passes instead of being folded into the preceding convolution. Each such kernel re-reads and re-writes the full NCHW/NHWC activation tensor. Because BatchNorm at inference is an affine transform with constant (frozen) running_mean/running_var/weight/bias, the scale and shift can be folded directly into the convolution weights and bias, deleting every one of these kernels and their full-tensor round trips.

evidence.kernel: triton_poi_fused__native_batch_1/2/3/4... (BatchNorm normalize + broadcast) and standalone elementwise activations

counters.bn_kernel_grid_dims: 1568 / 3136 / 12544 / [98,128] blocks, block 128-256, raw{} (metrics not captured but pure elementwise memory passes)

transformation.description: Conv-BatchNorm folding (inference). Match each (aten::convolution | aten::cudnn_convolution) -> aten::_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training (or aten::cudnn_batch_norm inference variant) chain in the Aten IR post-grad graph. Fold gamma/sqrt(var+eps) into the conv weight and (beta - gamma*mean/sqrt(var+eps)) into the conv bias, then rewrite all uses of the batch_norm output to the (now bias-carrying) convolution output and erase the batch_norm node plus its getitem nodes. This removes the separate normalization + broadcast Triton kernels entirely and shrinks the prologue to just the conv kernels.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), per CLAUDE.md memory note: use post_grad_custom_pre_pass on torch 2.11, NOT aot_autograd fw_compiler
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in (torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default, torch.ops.aten.cudnn_batch_norm.default): continue
if bn_input.op != 'call_function' or bn_input.target not in (torch.ops.aten.convolution.default, torch.ops.aten.cudnn_convolution.default): continue
if len(bn_input.users) != 1: continue   # conv output must feed only the BN
folded_w, folded_b = fold_conv_bn_weights(conv_w, conv_b, running_mean, running_var, weight, bias, eps)  # scale = gamma/sqrt(var+eps); w*=scale[:,None,None,None]; b=(b-mean)*scale+beta
result_user = next(u for u in node.users if u.target == operator.getitem and u.args[1] == 0)
transformation.code_hint: Standard conv-bn fold: scale = gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps); W_folded = W * scale.reshape(C_out,1,1,1); b_folded = (conv_bias - running_mean) * scale + beta. Guard on inference (no_training BN variant) and single-consumer conv output.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full read+write of every post-conv activation tensor per BN (the [98,128] grid kernel alone writes a 16x256x56x56 tensor). Removes ~all 'triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm' passes; conv-only prologue.

optimizations.notes: Highest-leverage change: the 50.9% prologue bucket is mostly BN/elementwise memory passes, not arithmetic. Folding is exact at inference. register_buffer is used for folded weights, so this pass must precede any dtype promotion (none proposed here) and must run before layout (OPT-2) and 1x1->mm (OPT-3) which key off the folded conv nodes. Confidence high: pattern is well-established and the BN params are frozen (no_training).

bottleneck.description: The pointwise (1x1) convolutions all map to the 'Kernel2' implicit-GEMM kernel, the single dominant kernel in the profile (dominant_kernel_id k_00056/k_00069/k_00082). It is catastrophically occupancy-starved: achieved_occupancy ~8.3% and sm_warps_active ~8.3% because launch__registers_per_thread = 224-228 and dynamic shared memory = 81.92 KB/block cap resident blocks per SM to ~1. With grid_dim only [784,1,1] (~5 waves on ~188 SMs) and eligible_cycles_pct ~16% (< 20 => latency-bound on Blackwell, where warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable), the SMs sit idle waiting on long-latency instruction chains. Tensor Cores run at only ~39-45%. A 1x1 convolution is exactly a GEMM: (N*H*W) x C_in @ C_in x C_out. Routing it through a proper cuBLAS GEMM (addmm) replaces this register-bloated implicit-GEMM kernel with a tiled, occupancy-balanced, fully Tensor-Core-engaged kernel.

transformation.description: Rewrite each 1x1 convolution (kernel_size [1,1], stride 1, no padding, groups=1) into reshape + aten::mm/addmm. For input x of shape [N,C_in,H,W] and weight [C_out,C_in,1,1]: permute to NHWC, view as [N*H*W, C_in], matmul with weight viewed as [C_in, C_out] (transposed), add folded bias (addmm), then view back to [N,C_out,H,W] and permute. This sends the work to cuBLAS, which on Blackwell selects a tiled GEMM with balanced occupancy and full Tensor-Core utilization instead of the 224-register implicit-GEMM 'Kernel2'.
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in (torch.ops.aten.convolution.default, torch.ops.aten.cudnn_convolution.default): continue
if meta is None or tuple(meta.shape[2:]) != (1,1): continue   # 1x1 only
mm  = gm.graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten.addmm.default, (bias, xf, wt)) if bias is not None else gm.graph.call_function(torch.ops.aten.mm.default, (xf, wt))
transformation.code_hint: 1x1 conv == GEMM. Use addmm with the (post-OPT-1) folded bias. Keep the permute pair adjacent to OPT-2's channels_last so the reshape is a no-copy view when the tensor is already NHWC-contiguous.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: No change in bytes moved; the win is occupancy (8% -> typical 50-70% for a cuBLAS GEMM) and full Tensor-Core engagement, roughly halving each Kernel2 latency. Estimate ~50% reduction on the 177920 ns of pointwise time.

optimizations.notes: Depends on OPT-1 (bias must already carry the folded BN shift before being passed to addmm). The duration_ns figures are 2-5x ncu-inflated so the 50% relative estimate is a within-profile comparison, not an absolute latency. Confidence medium: the substitution is mechanically sound but the realized speedup depends on cuBLAS heuristic selection for these small (N*H*W=50176 rows) GEMMs; validate against a kept-as-conv baseline.

bottleneck.description: 'triton_poi_fused_convolution_0' (grid [98,16,1], block 128) is a pure layout/copy kernel that converts the activation to the channels_last (NHWC) layout the cuDNN 'conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc' kernels require. It is launched repeatedly and is severely latency-bound: eligible_cycles_pct ~9.0, smsp__issue_active ~9%, sm_throughput ~9.6%, tensor_core_active 0, registers 25. It performs no math - it is overhead inserted because the graph carries NCHW tensors while every downstream conv kernel name is the *_nhwc variant. Forcing channels_last memory format end-to-end lets each conv consume its producer's output directly and removes these copies.

transformation.description: Propagate channels_last (NHWC) through the whole block stack so Inductor does not re-insert NCHW<->NHWC copies around each conv. Convert the model input once to torch.channels_last and ensure folded conv weights are stored channels_last; the per-conv 'triton_poi_fused_convolution_0' copy nodes then become identity and are eliminated. At the Aten IR level this is realized by removing redundant aten::clone(memory_format)/aten::_to_copy layout nodes whose input layout already matches consumer expectation, and by tagging the placeholder input as channels_last.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), after OPT-1; combined with eager-side x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) at model entry
fx_steps: # eager wrapper: feed inputs as channels_last so the first copy is amortized once
if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in (torch.ops.aten.clone.default, torch.ops.aten._to_copy.default): continue
if src_meta is not None and _stride_is_channels_last(src_meta):
node.replace_all_uses_with(src); gm.graph.erase_node(node)   # copy is a no-op
transformation.code_hint: Set the example input and module to .to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) before compile, then strip now-redundant layout-conversion nodes. Verify conv kernel names stay *_nhwc and that fused_convolution_0 disappears from the trace.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes the standalone full-tensor layout copies (each reads+writes the activation). Also lets depthwise conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc kernels read producer output with no reshuffle.

optimizations.notes: Depends on OPT-1 because the layout copies must be re-evaluated against the post-fold conv graph (some copies sit between conv and the now-deleted BN). Lower priority - only ~2.5% directly - but it also unlocks no-copy reshapes for OPT-3's permute pair. Confidence medium: the exact placement of the layout nodes depends on Inductor's lowering; verify the copies are truly redundant before erasing.

bottleneck.description: The activation (ReLU/ReLU6) following each BatchNorm is emitted as its own elementwise kernel rather than being fused into the conv/BN epilogue, adding extra full-tensor read+write passes inside the prologue. After OPT-1 folds BN into the conv, the activation can be fused into the conv epilogue (or the OPT-3 addmm epilogue) as a single pointwise op, removing the separate launch.

counters.note: activation kernels carry raw{} metrics; impact inferred from the launch chain and full-tensor memory cost

transformation.description: Fuse aten::relu / aten::clamp_min (ReLU/ReLU6) into the preceding folded convolution or the OPT-3 addmm by letting Inductor's pointwise scheduler attach it as an epilogue. At the custom-pass level, ensure the relu node's only producer is the conv/addmm output and leave it adjacent so Inductor fuses them into one Triton kernel. This removes the separate activation launch and its full-tensor round trip.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass (Aten IR), after OPT-1 (and ideally OPT-3)
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in (torch.ops.aten.relu.default, torch.ops.aten.clamp_min.default): continue
if prod.op == 'call_function' and prod.target in (torch.ops.aten.convolution.default, torch.ops.aten.addmm.default) and len(prod.users) == 1:
# keep nodes adjacent so Inductor fuses; no structural rewrite needed beyond ensuring single-consumer
gm.recompile()  # rely on Inductor pointwise fusion of the now-adjacent epilogue
transformation.code_hint: Often automatic once BN is folded (conv -> relu is a default Inductor fusion). Explicit only if a layout/copy node sits between conv and relu and blocks fusion; remove that node (see OPT-2).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes one full-tensor read+write per activation by folding it into the conv/GEMM epilogue.

optimizations.notes: Largely subsumed by OPT-1: once BN is folded and OPT-2 removes intervening layout copies, Inductor typically fuses conv->relu automatically. Listed separately so the activation passes are accounted for. Confidence low because the activation kernels carry empty raw{} metrics, so the exact saved time is inferred from the launch chain rather than measured. Depends on OPT-1 and benefits from OPT-2/OPT-3.

global_notes: knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repository; ASSUMPTION: used general Blackwell knowledge. RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition sm_count assumed ~188; Waves/SM derived from this (Kernel2 grid 784 => ~5 waves but capped at ~8% occupancy by registers/smem, so occupancy-limited not wave-limited). FX surgery patterns taken from torch.fx.Graph/Node standard APIs (insert_before, replace_all_uses_with, erase_node, eliminate_dead_code).
2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 1.37% of attributed time): aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=6 (0.68%) and op_id=33 (0.69%), both depthwise 3x3 32ch.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null across all operators (removed on Blackwell); latency-bound classification uses eligible_cycles_pct < 20. Kernel2 (16.3%), the pointwise BN passes, and triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 (9.0%) are all latency-bound; depthwise conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc (eligible ~40-45%, dram 60-77%) is memory-bound near roofline and is NOT a profitable target.

edge_case_flag dynamic_shapes: 'Kernel2' duration varies 13696-48480 ns (CV > 50%) for the same kernel_name. CAVEAT: this is not true dynamic shaping - it is one templated implicit-GEMM kernel reused across three static channel widths (32->64, 64->128, 128->256). Treat per-size, not as recompilation risk.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: the per-block 'aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=N' operators have is_fused=true (fused_with aten::_convolution/aten::convolution). Each is attributed once via NVTX to a single kernel; no double counting was added to the budget, but the prologue aggregate and the per-op aggregates describe overlapping graph regions (prologue is the deduped unique layer; the op_id entries are post-dedup per-conv ranges) - do not sum prologue + op_id entries as if disjoint workloads.

unattributed_kernels: 12 Triton kernels (k_00060..k_00083, the 'triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm' family) are unattributed (~18% of non-init kernels, below the 0.3 high_unattributed threshold). These are the same BN/elementwise passes OPT-1 targets; OPT-1's impact is therefore partly under-counted in the attributed budget.

All durations are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used only for relative ranking within this profile, never as absolute latencies. estimated_impact ns values are within-profile relative comparisons.

Dependency DAG (no cycles): OPT-1 -> {OPT-2, OPT-3, OPT-4}. OPT-1 (conv-BN fold) registers folded-weight buffers and must run first. OPT-3 (1x1 conv -> addmm) consumes the folded bias. OPT-2 (channels_last) re-evaluates layout copies against the post-fold graph and makes OPT-3's permute/reshape no-copy. OPT-4 (activation fusion) is mostly an automatic consequence of OPT-1 + OPT-2.
